meta.siteDescription: Licensed nurses for post-operative recovery, elderly daily care, wound care, injections, and IV therapy — delivered to your home in Beirut and Mount Lebanon.

footer.blurb: Licensed home nursing care in {area}. Founded and clinically supervised by a Lebanese BSN.

hero.subtitle: Licensed RNs for post-operative recovery, elderly daily care, wound care, injections, and IV therapy — in {area}.
hero.headline: Hospital-trained nurses, sent
hero.sub: Lebanese RNs and PNs working their hospital off-days. Coordinated by WhatsApp. A written summary in the family portal after every visit.

categories.heading: Hospital-grade care, at home
categories.subtitle: Clinical visits are performed by licensed RNs. Companion shifts are staffed by trained junior caregivers, supervised by our lead nurse. You get the right level of support — no more, no less.
categories.viewAll: See the full list of services

postOp.title: Post-Operative Care
postOp.description: Recover safely at home after surgery with nurses who follow your discharge plan.

elderly.title: Elderly Daily Care
elderly.description: Consistent, dignified support for elderly patients living at home.

ivWound.title: Injections, Wounds & IV
ivWound.description: Clinical procedures done at home — no hospital trip required.

junior.title: Companion & Junior Care
junior.description: Trained junior caregivers for non-clinical support, supervised by a lead RN.

howItWorks.headline: Three moments.
howItWorks.lede: A coordinator replies on WhatsApp, a nurse visits, a written summary lands in the family portal the same day. No phone tag.

steps.headline: Message us — in your own words.
steps.body: WhatsApp is the front door. A few lines is enough. EN · AR · FR. A coordinator replies the same day with what the visit needs.

steps.headline: A licensed nurse arrives — matched to the case.
steps.body: RN or PN, depending on the procedure. Same-nurse continuity when the case is ongoing.

steps.headline: A written summary, the same day.
steps.body: Vitals, what was done, what to watch for, next visit. In the family portal — visible to whoever you share it with.

whyChooseUs.heading: Four things we commit to
whyChooseUs.subtitle: Founded and clinically supervised by a Lebanese BSN. Each commitment here is something we keep — not marketing language.
whyChooseUs.pillar1Title: Verified with the Order of Nurses
whyChooseUs.pillar1Description: Every RN's active license is verified with the Lebanon Order of Nurses before their first visit. Junior caregivers must be recent graduates from accredited Lebanese programs.
whyChooseUs.pillar2Description: Send a WhatsApp message and you'll hear back within 30 minutes, every day from 7am to 11pm. Messages sent overnight get a reply by 7am.
whyChooseUs.pillar3Description: On long-term care plans, you get a dedicated primary nurse who knows your patient — not a different person each visit. No re-explaining the situation.
whyChooseUs.pillar4Description: After each visit you receive a written summary: vitals, what was done, anything the nurse noticed. No surprises, no information gaps.

serviceArea.headline: Beirut
serviceArea.lede: Districts where the coordinator currently has nurses available. If your district isn't listed, message us anyway — most off-map cases find a fit.
serviceArea.offMapBody: Message the coordinator anyway — most off-map cases find a fit.

ctaBanner.heading: Ready to talk to a nurse?
ctaBanner.subtitle: Message us with the patient's situation. Free 15-minute phone consult — no obligation, no hard sell.

diaspora.headline: Your parents are in Beirut.
diaspora.lede: Pay from Paris. Read the visit summary in Dubai. Approve the next visit from London. The portal works in three timezones at once.

services.headline: Care that comes
services.lede: Five services, plus a sixth for cases that don't fit a category. Every visit is matched by clinical need, not by package. No prices on the public site — quote on WhatsApp.

items.label: 01 · Clinical visits
items.headline: Injections, IV insertion, hydration, wound care.
items.body: IM/SubQ injection, IV start and medication administration, Foley insertion, complex dressing changes.

items.label: 02 · Recovery at home
items.headline: Post-surgical care after discharge.
items.body: Wound, drain, mobility, hand-off coordination with the operating team.

items.label: 03 · Daily care
items.headline: Daily care for an elder parent.

items.label: 04 · Bloodwork at home
items.headline: Routine, fasting, same-day samples.
items.body: Collected at home and walked to the lab.

catchAll.label: — Outside this list?
catchAll.headline: Not every case fits a category.
catchAll.body: Message the coordinator. Most off-list cases find a fit.

faq.headline: Questions, before we
faq.lede: If your question isn't here, the coordinator will answer it on WhatsApp the same day.
faq.helperBody: Message the coordinator — most replies land within a few hours during Beirut daytime.

items.question: Why don't you publish prices?
items.answer: High-touch service. Every case has a different shape. We quote on WhatsApp after a short conversation.

items.question: How does payment from abroad work?
items.answer: Western Union, OMT International, Whish Money, or direct USD wire. The coordinator sends you a summary of what's billed and how to pay in your timezone.

items.question: Can I read the visit summary in Arabic?
items.answer: Yes. The portal has EN, AR, FR. The summary is written in the language the coordinator and you agreed on.

items.question: What if it's 3am where I am?
items.answer: The coordinator replies in your daytime. The portal is always available. Emergencies still get same-day response in Beirut time.

items.question: How are the nurses vetted?
items.answer: Lebanese Order of Nurses licensed, currently employed at AUBMC, Hôtel-Dieu, or St Georges. Off-day shifts only. Background-checked.

items.question: Can I keep the same nurse week to week?
items.answer: Yes, when the case is ongoing. Same-nurse continuity is the default for daily care and recovery cases.

items.question: What ends up in the family portal?
items.answer: Vitals, what was done that visit, what to watch for, next visit date. Wound photos in a private bucket if applicable.

items.question: What's not offered yet?
items.answer: Live-in care, NG tube, ICU/tracheostomy specialty, Holter monitoring, oxygen rental. Coming later.

services.heading: What we offer
services.subtitle: Every visit covers travel within {area} and is performed by a licensed RN — except companion shifts, which are staffed by supervised junior caregivers. Need something not on this list? Message us — we likely do it.
services.ctaSubtitle: Tell us about the patient and we'll recommend the right service. Pricing is shared once we understand your situation.

categories.title: Post-Operative Care
categories.description: Safe recovery at home after surgery. Our nurses follow your discharge instructions and coordinate with your surgeon when needed.

categories.title: Elderly Daily Care
categories.description: Consistent support for elderly patients who need regular monitoring or assistance with daily medical tasks.
items: Vital signs check (BP, HR, O₂, temperature)

categories.title: Injections, Wound Care & IV
categories.description: Clinical procedures that usually require a hospital visit — performed at home by a licensed RN.

categories.title: Companion & Junior Care
categories.description: Trained junior caregivers — fresh graduates supervised by our lead RN — for support that doesn't require a full clinical visit. Lower cost, real attention.
items: Hospital sitter shifts (4-12 hr, day or overnight)
Companion presence so family can rest
Pediatric monitoring at home (sick child, post-op child, parent hospitalized)
Escalation to lead RN if patient deteriorates

categories.title: Long-Term Home Patients
categories.description: For patients who need ongoing care at home, we provide a dedicated nurse and keep clinical notes (vital trends, wound healing, medication tracking) so the family always has a clear picture.
items: Care plan coordinated with the treating physician
Wound healing tracked with progress photos
Regular updates shared with the family

packages.heading: Packages we offer
packages.subtitle: Pick the structure that fits the patient. Pricing is shared on a free 15-minute phone consult — once we understand the situation, we can quote accurately.
packages.ctaSubtitle: Tell us about the patient — current condition, what kind of help is needed, how often. We'll suggest the right package and share pricing in a free 15-minute phone consult.

single.title: One-off care, when you need it
single.description: A single visit for a specific need — no commitment, no plan. Best for one-time procedures or short-term care.
includes: Licensed RN visit (typically 30–60 min)
All travel within our service area
Required disposables and dressing supplies
Written visit summary sent to family
single.ctaMessage: Hi, I'd like to book a single visit.

weekly.title: Recurring scheduled visits
weekly.description: Predictable scheduled visits over weeks or months. Best for elderly daily care, ongoing wound care, or IV courses.
includes: Same nurse across visits where possible
Visit calendar shared with the family
Vital signs and medication log kept between visits
Direct line to a supervising RN between visits
Written summaries after every visit
weekly.ctaMessage: Hi, I'd like to set up a weekly care plan.

longTerm.title: Dedicated nurse, full clinical record
longTerm.description: For families managing a patient who needs ongoing daily care — your nurse becomes part of the family's care team.
bestFor: Patients with chronic conditions (Parkinson's, dementia, post-stroke)
Families who want continuity and detailed records
includes: Dedicated primary nurse who knows your patient
Care plan coordinated with the treating physician
Wound healing tracked with progress photos (with consent)
Medication and vital signs log shared with the family
Regular family updates by WhatsApp
Family Portal access (coming soon)
longTerm.ctaMessage: Hi, I'd like to discuss long-term primary nurse care.

companion.title: Trained company, lower cost
companion.description: A junior caregiver — recent BSN/LPN graduate, supervised by our lead RN — for non-clinical support over longer hours. Lower cost than an RN visit.
bestFor: Sick child while a parent is hospitalized
Companionship for an isolated elderly patient
includes: Trained junior caregiver, scope-limited to non-clinical tasks
Basic vital signs and intake/output charting
Supervised by our lead RN — on call by phone during the shift
Mobility, meal, and comfort assistance
Escalation pathway if patient deteriorates
companion.ctaMessage: Hi, I'd like to book a companion / sitter shift.

howWeWork.heading: How we work
howWeWork.subtitle: Trust in healthcare comes from process, not promises. Here is exactly how we vet our nurses, handle your request, and keep you informed at every step.
howWeWork.ctaHeading: Have a question about our process?
howWeWork.ctaSubtitle: Message us on WhatsApp or send a note. We'll answer honestly — even questions we don't love.

steps.title: How we vet our RNs
body: Active license verified directly with the Lebanon Order of Nurses before the nurse takes any case.
Minimum one year of clinical experience in a hospital, clinic, or established home-care setting.
Reference call with a previous supervisor or instructor.
In-person interview covering clinical scenarios — wound care, medication errors, escalation decisions.

steps.title: How we vet junior caregivers
body: Recent BSN or LPN graduate from an accredited Lebanese program (AUB, LAU, USJ, or equivalent).
Scope strictly limited to non-clinical work: companion shifts, monitoring, basic vitals, comfort care.
Every junior caregiver works under the supervision of our lead RN, who is on-call by phone during their shifts.
Same in-person interview process as RNs, focused on observation and escalation.

steps.title: What happens when you contact us
body: You message on WhatsApp — we reply within 30 minutes between 7am and 11pm. Overnight messages get a reply by 7am.
We offer a free 15-minute phone consult to understand the patient and what's needed.
Once we've agreed on the right service, we match a nurse and confirm the first visit time.
For most non-urgent requests, the first visit happens within 24 hours.

steps.title: Quality and safety
body: Every visit follows a documented clinical protocol — no improvising on procedures.
Each visit ends with a written summary sent to the family: vitals, what was done, anything we noticed.
If the nurse identifies a concern outside our scope, we escalate to the treating physician immediately.
Wound photos are taken with consent, stored privately, and never shared outside the care team.

steps.title: Privacy and dignity
body: Patient information is treated as confidential medical data. We do not log it in third-party tools.
Photos require written consent — and you can withdraw consent at any time.
Our nurses dress professionally, knock before entering rooms, and respect the home as the patient's private space.
We share clinical notes with you and the treating physician — no one else.

faq.heading: Questions families ask us
faq.subtitle: Direct answers to the questions families bring up most often. If your question isn't here, message us on WhatsApp — we'll add it.
faq.ctaSubtitle: Message us — every answer goes straight to a nurse, not a call center.

items.a: {area}, including Achrafieh, Hamra, Verdun, Ras Beirut, Mar Mikhael, Gemmayzeh, Badaro, Jdeideh, Antelias, Dbayeh, Jounieh, Baabda, Hazmieh, Mansourieh, and surrounding districts. If you're outside this list, message us anyway — we sometimes cover farther for long-term care.

items.q: How quickly can a nurse come to me?
items.a: For non-urgent requests, the first visit usually happens within 24 hours of confirmation. For urgent same-day visits we'll do everything we can; same-day is not always guaranteed but is often possible.

items.q: Are you available on weekends and at night?
items.a: Yes — we operate 7 days a week. WhatsApp messages between 7am and 11pm get a reply within 30 minutes. Overnight messages are answered by 7am. Visits can be scheduled any day, including overnight shifts for hospital sitters.

No. For emergencies (chest pain, severe bleeding, loss of consciousness, suspected stroke), call 140 (Lebanese Red Cross) or go to the nearest emergency room. We're a planned home-care service, not an emergency response.

items.q: How do I book a visit?
items.a: Message us on WhatsApp or fill in the contact form. We'll offer a free 15-minute phone consult to understand the patient and what's needed, then suggest the right service and confirm a time.

items.q: Why don't you publish prices on the site?
items.a: Pricing depends on the type of visit, duration, location within our service area, and whether it's a one-off or ongoing care. We share clear pricing during the free consult — no hidden fees, no surprise charges later.

items.q: What payment methods do you accept?
items.a: We accept cash and bank transfer. We're working on adding card payments. We confirm payment terms during the consult so there are no surprises.

items.q: Can I cancel or reschedule a visit?
items.a: Yes. Reschedules with at least 4 hours' notice are free. Last-minute cancellations may carry a small fee since we've already committed the nurse's time. Long-term care plans have their own cancellation terms shared at signup.

Every RN's active license is verified directly with the Lebanon Order of Nurses before their first case. Our junior caregivers are recent BSN or LPN graduates from accredited Lebanese programs, working under supervision and limited to non-clinical tasks.

items.q: Can the same nurse come every visit?
items.a: On long-term care plans, yes — you get a dedicated primary nurse who knows your patient. For one-off visits we'll usually try to send the same person if you book again, but it's not guaranteed.

items.q: What's the difference between an RN visit and a junior caregiver shift?
items.a: An RN handles clinical work — wound care, IV, injections, post-op assessment, catheter care, medication administration. A junior caregiver handles non-clinical support — sitting with a patient at the hospital or at home, taking basic vitals, helping with meals and comfort, and escalating concerns to our lead RN. Junior shifts cost less and cover longer hours.

Yes. A hospital sitter (junior caregiver) sits with your patient at the hospital — overnight or during the day — so the family can rest. They monitor the patient, help with meals and mobility, and call hospital staff or our lead RN if anything changes.

items.q: Will I get notes after each visit?
items.a: Yes. After every visit you receive a written summary: vitals taken, what was done, anything the nurse noticed, and any recommendations. For long-term patients we keep a running clinical record you can request anytime.

items.q: How do you protect patient information?
items.a: Patient information is treated as confidential medical data. We don't log it in third-party tools. Only the assigned nurse, the supervising RN, and the family see the records. We never share data with marketers, insurers without consent, or anyone outside the care team.

Wound photos are taken only with written consent and stored privately. They're used to track healing and shared only with the family and the treating physician. You can withdraw consent at any time and we'll delete the photos.

items.q: What if I'm not home — can the nurse still come?
items.a: Only with explicit prior arrangement. We don't enter a patient's home without a responsible adult present unless you've authorized it in advance and the patient is competent to receive care alone. Safety of patient and nurse comes first.

forNurses.heading: Join our nursing network
forNurses.subtitle: We're building a vetted nursing network in {area}. Whether you're an experienced RN or a fresh nursing graduate looking for supervised first-year work — we want to hear from you.

benefits.title: Flexible scheduling
benefits.description: You choose when you work. Accept cases that fit your availability — no fixed shifts.

benefits.title: Fair, direct pay
benefits.description: Competitive per-visit rates paid on time. No middleman taking a large cut.

benefits.title: Meaningful cases
benefits.description: Build real relationships with patients. Continuity of care — not a revolving door.

forNurses.tracksSubtitle: We hire at two levels — experienced RNs for clinical visits, and fresh graduates for supervised companion and monitoring shifts.

rnTrack.title: Licensed Registered Nurse
rnTrack.description: Clinical home visits — wound care, IV, post-op, injections, NGT, catheter care, and long-term primary nurse roles.
requirements: Active nursing license (Lebanon Order of Nurses)
Comfortable documenting and communicating findings to families
rnTrack.applyMessage: Hi, I'd like to apply as a licensed RN.

juniorTrack.title: Junior Caregiver — Fresh Graduate
juniorTrack.description: Companion shifts and pediatric monitoring. Supervised by our lead RN, scope-limited to non-clinical tasks. A great way for recent grads to build experience.
requirements: Recent BSN or LPN graduate from an accredited Lebanese program
Comfortable taking vital signs and observing patient status
Eager to learn — work alongside an experienced RN
juniorTrack.applyMessage: Hi, I'd like to apply as a junior caregiver.

forNurses.ctaSubtitle: Send us a message with your name, years of experience, and area of specialty. We'll suggest the right track and follow up within 24 hours.
forNurses.ctaMessage: Hi, I'd like to apply to your nursing network.

contact.heading: Contact us
contact.subtitle: The fastest way to reach us is WhatsApp. You can also fill in the form below and we'll reply shortly.

form.name: Your name
form.messagePlaceholder: Tell us about the patient — current condition, what kind of help is needed, when.
form.successBody: We'll reply within 30 minutes between 7am and 11pm. Overnight messages get a reply by 7am.
form.errorBody: Please try again or message us on WhatsApp.
form.phoneInvalid: Please enter a valid phone number
